# ccc — call coding CLIs

> One command to run any coding CLI. A unified interface implemented in 20 programming languages.

ccc is a command-line tool that wraps terminal-first coding agents (OpenCode, Claude Code, Codex, Kimi, Crush) behind a single consistent interface. Each of the 20 language implementations bundles an identical `ccc` CLI binary and a reusable library with `CommandSpec`, `CompletedRun`, and `Runner` types.
Python and Rust also expose `ccc --print-config`, which prints the canonical example config with the current supported keys, and `ccc config`, which prints the resolved config file path and contents.
Python and Rust also expose `ccc add [-g] <alias>`, a line-prompt alias wizard that writes `[aliases.<name>]` entries to config; scriptable flags plus `--yes` can write aliases non-interactively.

## Usage

ccc [controls...] "<Prompt>"

Slots are parsed in free order before the prompt, except that `-h` / `--help` wins anywhere in argv and prints help immediately:
- runner: Select which coding CLI (default: oc). Options: opencode (oc), claude (cc), kimi (k), codex (c/cx), roocode (rc), crush (cr)
- +thinking: Set thinking level from +0 (off) to +4 (max)
  Claude keeps the same `ccc` scale: `+0` becomes `--thinking disabled`, and `+1..+4` become `--thinking enabled` with matching `--effort`.
  Kimi keeps the same `ccc` scale: `+0` becomes `--no-thinking`, and `+1..+4` become `--thinking`.
- --show-thinking: Request visible thinking output when supported; default off. Implemented in Python and Rust for now.
- --save-session: Explicitly allow the selected runner's normal session persistence. Implemented in Python and Rust for now.
- --cleanup-session: Try post-run cleanup when the selected runner lacks a no-persist flag. Implemented in Python and Rust for now.
- --output-mode: Choose `text`, `stream-text`, `json`, `stream-json`, `formatted`, or `stream-formatted`
  Dot-sugar aliases are available before the prompt: `.text` / `..text`, `.json` / `..json`, `.fmt` / `..fmt`.
  Human-formatted modes honor `FORCE_COLOR` and `NO_COLOR` before falling back to TTY detection.
- :provider:model: Override provider and model
- @name: Use a named preset from the loaded config layers, or treat it as an agent when no preset exists; in Python and Rust, presets can also provide a default prompt when the user leaves prompt text blank, and alias `prompt_mode = "prepend"|"append"` can wrap explicitly supplied prompt text with alias prompt text
- Config: in Python and Rust, project-local `.ccc.toml` files are searched upward from the current directory, then `XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ccc/config.toml`, then `~/.config/ccc/config.toml`
- Generated config: `ccc --print-config` prints the canonical schema using `[defaults]`, `[abbreviations]`, and `[aliases.<name>]`
- Resolved config: `ccc config` prints the active config file path and its raw contents; `CCC_CONFIG` wins when it points at an existing file
- Alias wizard: `ccc add <alias>` writes the resolved config path; `-g` forces the effective global config instead of project-local config; blank/default answers omit alias keys

Session persistence:
- Python and Rust add Claude `--no-session-persistence` and Codex `--ephemeral` by default.
- OpenCode, Kimi, Crush, and RooCode may still save sessions by default; Python and Rust warn unless `--save-session` or `--cleanup-session` is used.
- `--cleanup-session` currently tries safe cleanup for OpenCode and Kimi only. Crush and RooCode warn because cleanup would require guessing.

Examples:
- ccc "Fix the failing tests"
- ccc --show-thinking "Fix the failing tests"
- ccc ..fmt cc +3 "Investigate the failing test"
- ccc .json oc "Reply with exactly ok"
- ccc cc +2 :anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-20250514 @reviewer "Add tests"
- ccc c +4 :openai:gpt-5.4-mini @agent "Debug the parser"
- ccc k +4 "Debug the parser"
- ccc --print-config
- ccc config
- ccc add mm27 --runner cc --prompt "Review changes" --prompt-mode default --yes

## Languages

Implemented in: Python, Rust, TypeScript, C, Go, Ruby, Perl, C++, Zig, D, F#, Haskell, Nim, Crystal, PHP, PureScript, Elixir, OCaml, VBScript, x86-64 ASM.

All implementations share: build_prompt_spec, CommandSpec, CompletedRun, Runner.run, ccc CLI, prompt trimming, empty prompt rejection, usage on bad args, exit code forwarding, and startup failure reporting.

Python and Rust also support real-runner override env vars for local smoke/debugging:
- `CCC_REAL_OPENCODE`
- `CCC_REAL_CLAUDE`
- `CCC_REAL_KIMI`

18 of 20 languages also implement: v2 parser/config (runner selector, thinking levels, provider/model override, preset and agent resolution, config loading) and v3 JSON output parsing (OpenCode, Claude Code, Kimi schemas).

## Docs

- [CLI Reference](https://xertrov.github.io/call-coding-clis/): Full usage, slots, examples, feature matrix, build and test instructions
- [JSON Event References](clis/json-event-references.md): upstream structured-output references for Claude, Kimi, and OpenCode
- [Mock Smoke Recipes](clis/mock-smoke.md): mock-runner smoke commands for formatted and raw output checks

## Optional

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/xertrov/call-coding-clis): Source code, all 20 language implementations
